programme.title: Wildest Middle East: Arabia: Land of Illusions
programme.desc: The Arabian peninsula is an ancient land of illusion. Extreme heat seems to sap life out of every corner. Yet, tricks of the light and the art of deception make survival possible. Nothing is as it seems.

programme.title: Wildest Islands: Sri Lanka: Monsoon Island
programme.desc: Lying like a teardrop in the Indian Ocean, Sri Lanka is an island that defies convention. Poised just six degrees above the equator, it's less than 75,000 km sq in size. But it has enough wildlife to rival a continent.

programme.title: Wildest Latin America: Pantanal: Brazil's Wild Heart
programme.desc: Every year the Pantanal is drowned by immense floods, and then suffer severe drought. Yet, while people struggle, the Pantanal hosts some of the greatest gatherings of animals on the planet.

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Cape Coast: Where Worlds Colide
programme.desc: Watch Africa's Cape Coast that goes from the frigid waters of Cape Point to the sub-tropic seas of Kwazulu-Natal.

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Rainforest
programme.desc: Daintree National Park is a magical meeting of rainforest and reef, lying at the heart of the world heritage listed wet tropics of Queensland.

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Madagascar: Islands of Monsters
programme.desc: Madagascar is truly a weird and wonderful land. Isolated on an island of extreme and magical looking locations, life has adapted and diversified. With few predators, numbers have proliferated and natural selection has gone mad.

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Ngorongoro: Born of Fire
programme.desc: Ngorongoro is thought to have formed about 2.5 million years ago from a large volcano, whose cone collapsed inward after a major eruption. This fertile land is full of wildlife, from cunning hyena and proud lions to vast herds of wildebeest and elephants.

programme.title: Wildest Latin America: Amazon: One Jungle, Many Worlds
programme.desc: The Amazon is the largest rainforest in the world, and the richest ecosystem on earth. This film uncovers what makes the Amazon such a powerhouse of evolution; how it has come to home a third of all species on the planet.

programme.title: Wildest Islands: Zanzibar: Land of Giants
programme.desc: The Zanzibar Archipelago has been labeled the most exotic island chain in the world. Pristine sands and azure blue sea make it a much loved holiday destination. /The Caribbean is a tropical paradise. Each of the 7,000 islands are unique.

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Okavango: Water in the Desert
programme.desc: The Okavango Delta, a huge emerald oasis in the burning heart of the Kalahari desert, is fed by the Okavango River and this perpetual cycle of wet and dry is the lifeblood of this extraordinary natural Eden.

programme.title: Wildest India: Thar Desert: Sacred Sands
programme.desc: Covering 200,000 km sq, India's Thar desert is one of the harshest places on Earth. Desiccating winds and near permanent drought has earned this unforgiving land another name: the region of death.

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: Iceland: Land of Fire and Ice
programme.desc: Iceland is a land born out of fire, sculpted by ice. This unique island is home to some of the planet's rarest and most spectacular creatures and it harbors some of the most extreme environments on Earth.
programme.sub-title: Iceland: Land of Fire and Ice

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Zambezi: Wild Water
programme.desc: The Zambezi is one of Africa's most influential rivers. It supports some of the continent's most spectacular wildlife. However, the Zambezi's long and winding journey destroys, as well as creates.

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Lake Turkana: Land of the Crocodile
programme.desc: The largest of Africa's Rift valley lakes, Lake Turkana is at the heart of one of the harshest landscapes on the planet. Despite its hostile environment, man and beast have both worked out a way to survive.
programme.sub-title: Lake Turkana: Land of the Crocodile

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Island of the Devil
programme.desc: Just 460,000 people live in Tasmania, an island off the southeast coast of mainland Australia. While its human population is small, the majestic state is rich in natural wonders and is known for its large tracts of pristine wilderness.

programme.title: Wildest India: The Himalayas: Surviving the Summits
programme.desc: The Himalayas run across 2,500km of north India. he Himalayas' rugged highlands offer little direct refuge to humans, in the shadow below, over a billion people in India rely on the mountains for survival.

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Congo: the Untamed Heart
programme.desc: Known for centuries as Africa's Dark Heart, the Congo is a multi-layered, three-dimensional world, seething with innumerable species. By peeling back the layers, we discover how this vast rainforest works, and expose the hidden lives of its inhabitants.

programme.title: Black Knight
programme.desc: When a wisecracking black hustler from South Central L.A. finds himself transported back to medieval England, he leads the overthrow of an evil king and wins the heart of a proto feminist woman.

programme.title: In Time
programme.desc: Justin Timberlake and Amanda Seyfried co-star in this sci-fi thriller set in a future world where aging stops at 25, and the only way for one man to stay alive is to steal more time.

programme.title: Labyrinth
programme.desc: Sixteen-year-old Sarah must solve a labyrinth to rescue her baby brother when he is taken by the Goblin King.

programme.title: The Ninth Gate
programme.desc: A rare book dealer, while seeking out the last two copies of a demon text, gets drawn into a conspiracy with supernatural overtones. Starring: Johnny Depp, Frank Langella, Lena Olin

programme.title: The Last Witch Hunter
programme.desc: The last witch hunter (Vin Diesel) is all that stands between humanity and the combined forces of the most horrifying witches in history.

programme.title: Jumper
programme.desc: A man discovers that he possesses the ability to instantly teleport anywhere in the world.

programme.title: Firehouse Dog
programme.desc: Hollywood's most pampered pooch gets lost during a commercial shoot and ends up as the mascot for a local fire station.

programme.title: Memoirs Of An Invisible Man
programme.desc: When a government experiment goes awry it renders an innocent bystander invisible.

programme.title: Evening
programme.desc: As Ann Lord reflects on one beautiful and life-changing weekend with the one true love of her life, her daughters come to their own understanding about the power of the past and the unbreakable bonds between mothers and daughters.

programme.title: Swamp Diamonds
programme.desc: An undercover police woman infiltrates a group of hardened female criminals who plan to break out of prison and retrieve diamonds from a swamp.

programme.title: The Phantom Planet
programme.desc: When US astronaut Captain Frank Chapman’s military spaceship is damaged by asteroids, he lands safely on the giant asteroid Rheton.

programme.title: Last Of The Wild Horses
programme.desc: A cowboy winds up in the middle of a turf war between a rancher and his neighbors in Last of the Wild Horses.

programme.title: Racket Girls
programme.desc: A plastic surgeon (played by Neil Connery, brother of Sean), the brother of a secret agent, combats villain Thair Beta.

programme.title: Blood Waters of Dr. Z
programme.desc: Seeking revenge against those responsible for stripping him of his tenure and the denial of human research subjects, Dr. Z mutates himself into a hideous — and murderous — half-human-half-fish-like creature!

programme.title: Cave Dwellers
programme.desc: Newly-married wife Jenni can't tell if the skull she sees in her new home is a ghostly apparition of her husband's dead first wife, hallucinations from her own nervous condition... or something else.

programme.title: The Rebel Set
programme.desc: Three coffee shop customers help an owner rob an armored car.

programme.title: Hercules Unchained
programme.desc: While trying to resolve a quarrel between warring brothers Eteocles and Polynices, Hercules is taken captive by Queen Omphale of Lydia. Can he escape from the royal court and rescue the woman he loves, before it’s too late?

programme.title: Indestructible Man
programme.desc: In Indestructible Man, a violent criminal named Butcher Benson is executed and becomes indestructible when he’s brought back to life. Butcher seeks revenge against those who caused his execution.

programme.title: Girl in Gold Boots
programme.desc: A girl from rural California goes to Los Angeles to work as a go-go dancer, but soon gets involved with drug dealers.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pranks for Nothing / Zombie Dearest
programme.desc: Eager to regain his lost prank mojo, McGee releases a mischievous pixy named Hanky Pranky.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Mc Crossroads / Lucky Duck
programme.desc: McGee signs a contract with Sheba the bandshee.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Who's Ghouling Who? / The Adominable Dr. Squatch
programme.desc: McGee accidentally summons Percy, a ghost with a very 'fiery' personality.
programme.sub-title: Who's Ghouling Who? / The Adominable Dr. Squatch

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Ants In Our Camp / Fanboy Freakout
programme.desc: McGee mistakenly concocts a spray that shrinks the campers to a microscopic size.
programme.sub-title: Ants In Our Camp / Fanboy Freakout

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Stage Fright / Frankenfixer
programme.desc: The Bottom Dwellers stage Armand's awful play but a jealous Suzi unleashes a curse on the production.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Bird Brains / The Real Vincent
programme.desc: Squirt is eager to assist his idol, Dr. Sapstein on his latest quest for the rare mocker bird.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Hiccups / Boogeyman Fever
programme.desc: Things start disappearing around Lakebottom!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Clockwork Slime / Ride the Haunted Howler
programme.desc: The Campers find a time travelling clock on the beach!
programme.sub-title: Clockwork Slime / Ride the Haunted Howler

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Valley of the Iguanasquat / Legend of Wiggly's Gold
programme.desc: When the campers discover a prehistoric valley near Stinky Pits they also find a cursed gem.
programme.sub-title: Valley of the Iguanasquat / Legend of Wiggly's Gold

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Adventures in Bottomland / Golem My Way?
programme.desc: During an unfair polo match, McGee falls down an armadillo and wakes up in 'Bottomland.'
programme.sub-title: Adventures in Bottomland / Golem My Way?

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Bloody Marty / Ghost In The Mower
programme.desc: The kids ignore Rosebuds warning and summon Bloody Marty from the ominous mirror world.
programme.sub-title: Bloody Marty / Ghost In The Mower

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Lakebo-tron / Rise of the Dawn of the Beginning of the Planet of Armand!
programme.desc: McGee and Buttsquat go head-to-head in an 80's style arcade game! But as the points build up, the boys become digitized inside the game. The kids head back through the Lakebottom inter-dimensional portal, hoping to find a Sasquatch version of the camp.
programme.sub-title: Lakebo-tron / Rise of the Dawn of the Beginning of the Planet of Armand!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Eclipsalypso / Apocalypse Squirt
programme.desc: McGee summons the Moon Dervish who will bring the Apocalypse unless a sasquatch dances for him.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: It's a Headless Horse, Man / Voyage to the Bottom of the Deep
programme.desc: McGee is thrilled when he wins a mounted horse head.
programme.sub-title: It's a Headless Horse, Man / Voyage to the Bottom of the Deep

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Escape from Lakebottom
programme.desc: McGee, Gretchen and Squirt arrive at Camp Lakebottom to find themselves held captive by a zombie.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Butt-Squad / You Sank My Battle-Squirt!
programme.desc: Buttsquat steps in to help the bottomdwellers when they suddenly acquire the same red hair and freckles as their Sunny Smiles nemesis. When an alien invasion threatens Camp Lakebottom, the kids defend the camp against the alien mothership!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Bite of the Buttsquat / Sword of Ittibiticus
programme.desc: No one believes McGee's suspicion that the suddenly ultra-cool Buttsquat is a campyre.
programme.sub-title: Bite of the Buttsquat / Sword of Ittibiticus

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Itchy Witchy Pizza Project / Breakout From Buttvault B
programme.desc: Gretchen, McGee and Squirt get trapped by witch who forces the campers into pizza slave labour.
programme.sub-title: Itchy Witchy Pizza Project / Breakout From Buttvault B

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Red Drawn / Pandora's Jock
programme.desc: A travelling salesman visits Lakebottom one boring rainy day and sells McGee a magic pencil.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Slaybells / Smells Like Holidays
programme.desc: The kids almost ruin the biggest monster holiday of the year!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: McGee the Mermaid / Welcome to Buttcon
programme.desc: The kids accidentally resurrect an evil mermaid who floods the camp.
programme.sub-title: McGee the Mermaid / Welcome to Buttcon

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Buttastic Journey / Monkey See, Monkey Kung Fu
programme.desc: McGee is shrunk down to the size of an atom!
programme.sub-title: Buttastic Journey / Monkey See, Monkey Kung Fu

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: The Superfantastic Mega-Buds / It Came From My Nose
programme.desc: McGee, Gretchen and Squirt get super fantastic mega powers!
programme.sub-title: The Superfantastic Mega-Buds / It Came From My Nose

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: S.P.U.D.S / Monster Hunters "R" Us
programme.desc: McGee, Gretchen and Squirt disturb the potato King!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Messy Bessie / Nanny Num Nums
programme.desc: The campers build a race car to help Sawyer relive his pro-racing days.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Late Afternoon of the Living Gitch / Mindsuckers from the Depths
programme.desc: When Squirt's stinky underwear is washed in toxic cleanser, the gitch comes alive.
programme.sub-title: Late Afternoon of the Living Gitch / Mindsuckers from the Depths

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Last Days / Knock on Wood
programme.desc: McGee tries to save Camp Lakebottom from being overtaken by the Grim Camper.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: McGee.T / The Great Tiki Hunt
programme.desc: McGee befriends a frog-like alien creature that crash lands close by Camp Lakebottom.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Schwampbillies / Remember Fort Sunny Bottom
programme.desc: When McGee, Gretchen and Squirt find themselves lost deep in the schwamp.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Cluck of the Were-Chicken / Gnome Force
programme.desc: When a hulking were-chicken stalks the camp, McGee must lead the hunt to prove his innocence.
programme.sub-title: Cluck of the Were-Chicken / Gnome Force

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Slugfight / Izomborg
programme.desc: The campers must travel back in time to prevent Grandpappy Black Butt from stealing the deed.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Head Two Head / The Last of the Wild
programme.desc: Suzi and Gretchen get fused into a two-headed Gruzi!
programme.sub-title: Head Two Head / The Last of the Wild

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Sweat, Hot Lakebottom Summer / The S'Mogre
programme.desc: A heat wave forces McGee to sneak a dab of Sawyer's anti-sweat cream. After trying to cheer up Armand, McGee creates a s'more so big, it topples and coats him in delicious goo.
programme.sub-title: Sweat, Hot Lakebottom Summer / The S'Mogre

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Seven Foot itch / Being McGee
programme.desc: Squirt ressurects his pet lousy from the grave.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Camp Plantbottom / Game Over
programme.desc: It's Suzi's birthday and McGee ate her gift!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: McGee's First Flush / House of Ear Wax
programme.desc: Two of McGee and Suzi's former pets "Ocean Apes" return for vengeance and are mutated in all kinds of horrible ways! Squirt's chronic ear wax issues escalate as his giant ball of wax seems to take on a mind of its own.
programme.sub-title: McGee's First Flush / House of Ear Wax

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Ice Queen / Live and Let Squatch
programme.desc: When the campers try to reclaim their stolen Lakebottom items.
programme.sub-title: Ice Queen / Live and Let Squatch

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Marshmallow Madness / 28 Suzi's Later
programme.desc: Armand goes into a feeding frenzy when wild marshmallows give the campers a rash.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Before they leave for New York, Paris and Nicole stay with the family of a former beauty queen, who convinces Paris to take part in a pageant, while their internship is at a dentist's office.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole take the place of a stay-at-home mom who has a young daughter, and also happens to be nine months pregnant!

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole trade in their designer dresses for traditional Pakistani attire when they take over the responsibilities of a Pakistani housewife.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole fill in for a housewife who feels her marriage is lacking a spark.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole spend the day tending to the needs and whims of four rambunctious kids.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole plan competing weddings for a lesbian couple.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole must take care of a toddler, as well as throw a party for his second birthday.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole take a break from household chores and go on a family vacation.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole are paired with a hard-to-please husband.

programme.title: The Simple Life

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris can’t stand to spend another day as a housewife and hires an impersonator to take her place, giving Nicole an opportunity to exact her revenge.

programme.title: Mob Wives: Staten Island Sea Hags
programme.desc: The fiery feud between Karen and Natalie continues to reach new heights causing irreparable damage in the Staten Island circle.

programme.title: Mob Wives: Forgive and Fuggedaboutit
programme.desc: Renee throws a party for the one year anniversary of her fashion line where a surprise guest shows up. Drita prepares her family for a long overdue vacation while also playing middle man between enemies.

programme.title: Mob Wives: Deck the Brawls
programme.desc: Drita isn't entirely sold on the new Natalie and makes a decision once and for all which Philly friend she intends to be loyal to.

programme.title: Mob Wives: Baptisms and Betrayals
programme.desc: Nat D's boyfriend makes a chilling and unexpected appearance at her home. Meanwhile, Renee decides to take her newfound peace to the next level. Karen receives a letter from her father causing her to work harder towards his early release from prison.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 13
programme.desc: Ed discovers his girlfriend's sordid affair with the unsuspecting Scott. Follow up with Johnny Burkhalter.

programme.title: Undercover Boss Canada
programme.desc: Canlan Ice Sports Corp is the largest private owner of sports facilities in North America. CEO Joey St Aubin admits their prices are higher than local municipal rinks, but believes his company offers a premium service and better ice quality.

programme.title: Undercover Boss Canada
programme.desc: Purdy’s dominates the West Coast, but is struggling to make an impression elsewhere. To boost sales, the Chocolate Queen travels the country, working in disguise in her own stores and factory floors.

programme.title: Undercover Boss Canada
programme.desc: This episode nets an exclusive “behind the scenes” look at what it really takes to get fish on our dinner plates.

programme.title: Undercover Boss Canada
programme.desc: The Victorian Order of Nurses takes the pressure off hospitals by providing care for people in their own homes. Hundreds of rival companies compete for cash and customers.

programme.title: Undercover Boss Canada
programme.desc: Tervita specializes in environmental clean-up for Western Canada’s booming energy and mining industries. It doesn’t take long for Fory Musser,

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 197
programme.desc: Can you jump to safety from a collapsing rope bridge as seen in the film "Cliffhanger"? Can a strong enough hailstorm sink a boat?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 182
programme.desc: Can C4 be used as a cooking fuel? Can you make a throwing weapon that could decapitate an opponent and carry the head back to the thrower?

programme.title: Mythbusters Hollywood Special
programme.desc: It’s lights, camera, action on this special episode of "MythBusters" as Adam and Jamie go Hollywood and pull the curtain back on a few brand new myths straight off the silver screen.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 142
programme.desc: Can antacid tablets be used to blow a prison cell open? Can a person drive safely on a moonless night without using his headlights?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 89
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is it possible to walk on water? Can a speeding arrow be snatched out of the air? Can a sword be stopped with one's bare hands?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 203
programme.desc: Can a motorcycle traveling at highway speeds drive across the surface of a lake? Can you survive a jump from a high-rise building with a parachute made from a hotel room's contents?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 3
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a barrel of bricks from the top of a building hit a man multiple times? Can a person be electrocuted by urinating on the third rail?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 57
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a whirlpool really kill somebody? Can a snow plough driving down the road push enough air to flip a passing car over?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 110
programme.desc: Does cabin fever exist? Does accelerating into a moose cause less damage than braking? Can a single stick of dynamite with a 20-second fuse be thrown, retrieved by a dog and detonate under an SUV?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 95
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a person's eyes pop out while sneezing with eyes open? Can shifting to reverse stop a runaway car? Can three cigarette butts fired from a black-powder rifle kill a person?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 104
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a person with no flight training land a jet while being assisted by the traffic controllers over the radio? Is it possible to talk while falling at terminal velocity?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 85
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is there any way to prevent a bloodhound from tracking one's scent? Is it possible to get past a guard dog? Can an old dog be taught new tricks?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 113
programme.desc: Can a ninja catch an arrow mid-flight? Could a ninja hide underwater breathing through a bamboo reed long enough to attack an enemy with a poison blow-dart?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 30
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a car pole-vault from its drive shaft if it hits a pothole? Can a port-a-potty gather enough methane to explode?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 199
programme.desc: Is the method used for boarding planes by most airlines inefficient? Can you make an untraceable bullet out of teeth?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 64
programme.desc: Myths tested: If a steel cable under high tension snaps, could it cut a human in half? Can sounds and vibrations be recorded into the grooves of ancient pottery?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 83
programme.desc: Myths tested: How can someone escape from a sinking car? Can paper be folded in half more than seven times?

programme.title: The Internship
programme.desc: Old-school salesmen Billy (Vince Vaughn) and Nick (Owen Wilson) try to reboot their careers by joining Google’s fiercely competitive internship program — with hilarious results!

programme.title: Life or Something Like It
programme.desc: The perfect world of a beautiful Seattle television reporter is shattered by the prediction of her death in seven days.

programme.title: Empire Records
programme.desc: A group of young, anti-establishment Gen-X employees fight to save their record store from being overtaken by a conglomerate retail record corporation.

programme.title: Freddy Got Fingered
programme.desc: A man is forced to move back in with his parent's and then refuses to leave.

programme.title: Simply Irresistible
programme.desc: Magic and laughter are the main ingredients in this SIMPLY IRRESISTIBLE love story of a restaurant owner/chef and a department store executive who seem to have nothing in common.

programme.title: Bandidas
programme.desc: In turn-of-the-century Mexico, two very different women become a bank-robbing duo in an effort to combat a ruthless enforcer terrorizing their town.

programme.title: King Of California
programme.desc: Commercial airline pilot Whip Whitaker (Denzel Washington) has a problem with drugs and alcohol, though so far he's managed to complete his flights safely.

programme.title: Guess Who
programme.desc: A sarcastic father (Bernie Mac) has plenty to say about his daughter wanting to marry a white boy (Ashton Kutcher).

programme.title: Superhero Movie
programme.desc: An orphaned teenager is bitten by a radioactive dragonfly, granting him superhuman powers in this parody of films like Spider-Man, X-Men, and Fantastic Four.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Transylvanian Homesick Blues
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny take a rollercoaster ride through time...literally!

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold go to the Amazon jungle where a tribe have been sacrificing people to The Great Ant God from Ataxia. Penfold is captured by the tribe and his "magic caterpillars" (eyebrows) are to be sacrificed.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Sawdust Ring
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny join the circus.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Town Hall Terrors
programme.desc: Duckula goes to the Town Hall to get a grant for his crumbling castle.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Crumhorn invents a chemical that allows him to create land sharks to terrorize London. Things get even more complicated when Penfold eats some of the formula.

programme.title: Count Duckula: There are Werewolves at the Bottom of Our Garden
programme.desc: The castle's werewolf has escaped and it's up to Igor and Nanny to get it back before Duckula finds out.
programme.sub-title: There are Werewolves at the Bottom of Our Garden

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Washing machines from all over London have busted out of their residences and are now under the control of Baron Greenback, who orders them to assemble and to attack Danger Mouse.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Igor's Busy Day
programme.desc: An engaged couple spend the night in Castle Duckula, allowing Igor to relive his good old days

programme.title: Count Duckula: A Mountie Always Gets His Duck
programme.desc: Duckula travels to Canada to become a Mountie.
programme.sub-title: A Mountie Always Gets His Duck

programme.title: Count Duckula: O.O. Duck
programme.desc: The Egg decides to steal Castle Duckula to take over the world.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The Mark III runs out of fuel, leaving DM and Penfold stranded in the Sahara Desert.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback has stolen a gem that is used to shoot a ray beam at people which results in them having extremely bad luck. Danger Mouse manages to get it and attempts to give it back to the original owners who do not really want it back.
programme.sub-title: The Bad Luck Eye of the Little Yellow God

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K teams Danger Mouse up with a robot Penfold, and Penfold with a robot Danger Mouse. The robots prove to be a lot of trouble for the real ones, taking instructions too literally or getting stuck running in circles.
programme.sub-title: Four Heads Are Better Than Two

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold head to outer space where they find a station has been beaming darkness onto the Earth. On the station they encounter a fearsome gremlin which operates on reverse logic.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Incredible Shrinking Duck
programme.desc: Von Goosewing tries shrinking the castle to put in a glass snowglobe model.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold travel to Wales where they must deal with a fire-breathing dragon. It turns out to be a dragon named Jones who is about the same size as them.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback kidnaps Penfold to lure Danger Mouse into a spaceship and send both of them to a planet ruled by old fashioned looking machines.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are on holiday in Spain. The delusional Don Coyote kidnaps Penfold and forces him to act as his assistant, 'Sancho Penfold'.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold travel to Australia where they pick up top secret plans from an agent rat in the form of a rugby ball. Penfold gets distracted by a boomerang and is later picked up by large bird of prey.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Inside the Royal Albert Hall, Greenback reveals his new invention: a duplication machine he plans to use to make hundreds of Greenbacks so that no-one would find the real one.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse finds out he needs to hurry to attend an award ceremony, but the Mark III somehow cops an attitude and refuses to be controlled. Danger Mouse and Penfold spent most of the episode trying to chase the Mark III down.
programme.sub-title: What a 3 Point Turn-up for the Book

programme.title: Count Duckula: Mississippi Duck
programme.desc: Count Duckula and his servants travel onto a steamboat in the River Mississippi.

programme.title: Count Duckula: A Christmas Quacker
programme.desc: Duckula is visited by both Father Christmas and Von Goosewing at the Castle on Christmas Day.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold meet a space alien who has crash-landed in Birmingham and who has a pet tickle-hi-puss that likes to crawl up Penfold's trouser legs. However, the pet gets loose and whenever there is some physical impact, it multiplies.

programme.title: Count Duckula: No Yaks Please, We're Tibetan
programme.desc: The trio travel to Tibet

programme.title: Count Duckula: Mutinous Penguins
programme.desc: Lost in the Arctic seas, Duckula, Igor and Nanny must survive in order to find their home.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dr Crumhorn returns and uses his transformation pills to turn himself into a little girl in an attempt to persuade Danger Mouse and Penfold to help her out and eventually lead them to break into Fort Knox.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: During an investigation where Danger Mouse and Penfold try to find out why elephants are being turned into sugar cubes, a fall causes Penfold to act weird and do a Tarzan yell that summons elephants to stampede the area.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When Danger Mouse drinks milk that has been drugged with a sedative, he feels very weak and groggy, and thus Penfold must lead the charge to fight El Loco, a Mexican bandit, who intends to rob the Bank of England.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback offers to team up with Danger Mouse in order to thwart an alien invasion. Professor Sqawkencluck outfits the Space Hopper with a secret weapon.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has placed a large number of sand pyramids all over England, threatening to sink the island Danger Mouse travels to the Sahara Desert to stop him.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Rent a Butler
programme.desc: Duckula sells Igor and Nanny to an agency to make some money

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The Earth is flooded with instant custard. Danger Mouse and Penfold travel to outer space to find the Custard Mite of Glut, a small creature that can eat up the custard, but the planet that houses the Mite is controlled by armoured aliens.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Show Must Go On
programme.desc: Duckula decides to become an actor and stages the story of Jack and the Magic Walnut at the castle.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are pursuing Baron Greenback when Danger Mouse hits his head and suffers amnesia.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are sent to find Merlin's magic inkwell which will grant the finder a wish. They go on a treasure hunt by following a series of clues.
programme.sub-title: Where There's a Well There's a Way

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold had to the China seas to look for Professor Squakencluck who, despite having invented an object that can never be lost, ends up dropping the item in a remote island and getting lost himself.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Down Under Duckula
programme.desc: While in Australia, Duckula accidentally sells the clock to a wallaby owner.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: While Penfold is home with a cold, Danger Mouse gets a mission to deal with the Mongol hordes that have recently acquired motorbikes.
programme.sub-title: The Spy Who Stayed In with a Cold

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Unpacking from a holiday in Mexico, Penfold discovers El Loco hiding in his suitcase. El Loco gets loose and goes on a crime spree in London.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold track Greenback down to a New York City skyscraper run by International Oddbods and a guy who wears a bag over his head. Inside, there are all sorts of bizarre things going on.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dr Crumhorn holds Penfold captive and has created a robot duplicate of him (which is far more efficient than the original). At the push of a button, it transforms into a giant machine intent on destroying Danger Mouse and his pillar box.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold travel to Scotland to keep Greenback from destroying the world with music from all the bagpipes in the world.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Dr. Goosewing & Mr Duck
programme.desc: Goosewing creates a formula where when drunk, the victim becomes the complete opposite of their original selves.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold have a day off, but they use it to go through the Amazon jungle to look for the long-lost crown of Questzebottle, an Aztec king who is known for inventing the practical joke.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Nero gains strange powers (telekenesis), which, he uses to terrorize Danger Mouse and Penfold, after he falls into one of Greenback's inventions.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Duck Ahoy
programme.desc: Duckula enters a boat race hoping to win the prize money and as usual, things don't go to plan.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 11
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a \\"land of opportunity\\" in Kent, the grade 2 listed property in Berkshire and a railway cottage in West Yorkshire that's on track to be a winner.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a plot of land in Hampshire, an end-of-terrace cottage in Derbyshire and a dingy one-bedroom flat in London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 18
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a property in Stoke-on Trent with a tiny kitchen, a block of bedsits in London and revisit a 400 year old listed house in Wiltshire.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 1
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a property in Cornwall, a 1950's property in Middlesex and a house in Wales.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 36
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander & Martin Roberts visit a mid terraced house in Tunbridge, a few rooms up for sale in Torquay and a two bedroomed apartment above a Barbers shop in East London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 9
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a flat in London, a mysterious house in Newcastle- Under-Lyme and a property in the seaside town of Ramsgate with a water problem.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 25
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a tired and dated semi in Telford, a flat in Margate and return to a magnificent 16 th century house in Derbyshire we first visited 4 ½ years ago.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 9
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a semi detached house in Wootton Bassett, Wiltshire, a three bed terrace in Tonbridge, Kent and a house in Dawley, Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 30
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a house in Dorset that's full of surprises, a property on Portobello Road in London and a cottage in Newquay.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 8
programme.desc: Lucy and Martin are hooked on a modern Welsh property, in Kent the clues look good for a Sidcup semi and in the basement of a Harrogate house there's a very cramped one bed flat.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 24
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a four bed end-of-terrace in need of modernisation in Greater Manchester, a studio apartment in Notting Hill and a cottage in Devon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 2
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a flat in south London that could benefit from more than just cosmetic surgery, a house in a prime location in Herne Bay and a mid terrace near a main road in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 10
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a property in Maida Vale, a former stable in Wiltshire and two bed semi in Lincolnshire.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit properties in Warrington, Dorset and Wales.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 12
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a flat in London, a house in Kent and a house in Greater Manchester.

programme.title: The Shape of Things to Come

programme.title: Laurel & Hardy: Another Fine Mess

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A rifle in a shopping mall, a syringe of potassium chloride and a syringe of insulin, each in the hands of a mentally disturbed woman, lead to the tragic end of many lives.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love Gone Wrong
programme.desc: Love can be beautiful, love can be blind and love can be deadly. Desire and jealousy are powerful forces driving women to dark and horrifying places in their minds.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The mind of a female killer is shaped by a complex cocktail of influences. Do people become that way, driven by horrendous events in their lives? Or are some killers born bad?

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Greed is one of the oldest motives for murder, and these deadly women are no exception: they’ll do anything to satisfy their need for cash. This is the dangerous world of the fortune hunters.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Lover's Revenge
programme.desc: These women burn madly with anger so deep they'll kill to satisfy it. What makes them turn on the ones they love? Find out as we explore the reasons for a lover's revenge.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Sins of the Sister
programme.desc: A sisterly bond can mean a life full of friendship and sibling love. But for some women, having a sister means nothing more then having their own personal meal ticket, scapegoat and combatant. These types of relationships can lead to deadly outcomes.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Revenge is one of the oldest motives for murder, but these cases are much more than an “eye for an eye.”

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These deadly women were consumed by obsessions that overtook their minds and their lives. These women all had a fatal fixation.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Killer Kids
programme.desc: For many parents, their children's teen years can be one big headache. But when typical teenage rebellion turns into deadly rage, a parent's love may not be enough to save their life.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Kill For Cash
programme.desc: Money is a strong motivator. It can be used for good. It can be used for evil. It can be the driving force behind murder. These women would stop at nothing to get their hands on the cash they needed.

programme.title: Deadly Women: A Daughter's Revenge
programme.desc: Every day, teenage girls are being scolded by their parents, only to respond with tears and slammed bedroom doors. Some daughters won’t take "no" for an answer, and these girls become deadly.

programme.title: Deadly Women: To Love and to Murder
programme.desc: Marriage is a lifelong commitment of love, and wedding vows. 'To have and to hold; for better, for worse; until death do us part'. But for these monstrous wives, it was only the vow of 'death do us part' they upheld.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The path towards revenge can sometimes lead to murder. It can be quick, fiery and violent. It can also be a dish served cold, the product of a calm, calculated plot.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Obsession
programme.desc: Deciphering what causes minds to corkscrew out of control is the complex work of the criminal profiler. Obsessions can take many forms: beauty, devotion, ego and power.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Judi Buenoano secretly murdered lovers to claim on insurance policies whilst nurse Genene Jones secretly killed at least 46 infants left in her care between 1980 and 1982.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Under His Control
programme.desc: These are love stories with bleak, bloody endings. Blinded by desire, obsessed and malleable, some women fall into lethal relationships with dangerous, controlling men.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women burn madly with jealousy, an anger so deep they’ll kill to satisfy it. What makes them turn from “green with envy” to “lethal with jealousy?" Find out as we explore their hearts of darkness.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women never get their hands dirty: no blood touched them because they manipulate others to kill for them...but they didn't get away.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: In some cases, the transformation from everyday woman to brutal killer is activated by a man. A boyfriend or husband can become the focus of a deadly obsession.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Cat and Mouse
programme.desc: He was a predator, abducting girls and young women from their own front yards in broad daylight. He was a sadist: phoning one victim's family, taunting them, boosting then dashing their hope, and he wouldn't stop until we stopped him.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Evil Intent
programme.desc: Drops of blood and a torn screen were all that's left to tell authorities what happened to a woman in Indiana. The 21 yr. old victim was taken from her home on a spring morning. She appeared to have no real enemies, there was no clear motive.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Trail
programme.desc: The disappearance of young women triggered a search that spanned the Pacific Northwest. A massive media campaign failed to locate the drifter, who first charmed then killed them. As investigators followed his trail, he always stayed one step ahead.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Firefight
programme.desc: In Miami, a gang of robbers targeted banks and armored cars. They carried assault weapons and weren't afraid to use them. They struck during the day, and sometimes twice a day. For awhile, they seemed unstoppable.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Moving Target
programme.desc: When a sniper in Utah started targeting African-American men for death, authorities launched a massive investigation. With scant clues and no witnesses they found the odds stacked against them, and they had no idea where the killer would strike next.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Secrets
programme.desc: Two young boys were the only witnesses to their mother's murder. The horrible crime appeared unmotivated, senseless and random.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Bank robbery is a brazen yet simple crime. A mask, a gun, a note passed to a teller. But bank burglary is different. It takes skill to disable an alarm, to penetrate a locked vault.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Death In The Delta
programme.desc: When a wealthy young woman was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The 25-year-old victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband and old boyfriends. All were on the FBI's short list.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Driven to Kill
programme.desc: A serial rapist targeted women. His dark urges raged out of control and led him to murder. He followed a path so random it was impossible to trace, and all that tied him to his crimes were his access to the highways and a bizarre sexual fetish.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Manhunt
programme.desc: A killer was on the loose in one of America's most popular tourist destinations. Armed and desperate, he evaded law enforcement at every turn. As the Fourth of July weekend quickly approached, thousands of people became potential targets.

programme.title: The FBI Files: No Remorse
programme.desc: A burning body in a dark alley shed light on a cold-blooded killer. His partners in crime would tell the story, if they lived. In the underworld of the drug trade, even a childhood friend can quickly become an enemy.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Betrayed
programme.desc: On the day before Thanksgiving, a mother discovered her ten-year-old son was missing from his school. When a child of separated parents disappears, investigators first look at the family and divorced parents often suspect each other.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Terror In Disguise
programme.desc: Banks use a variety of devices to discourage would-be robbers: security cameras, silent alarms, and exploding dye packs. But when an elusive bank robber targeted Seattle's banks, none of these precautions were effective.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Paradise
programme.desc: It should have been Shangri-La, but when bad blood developed, piracy took over and the only law was the law of the jungle.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dixie Mafia
programme.desc: On Mississippi's Gulf Coast, a Judge and his politician wife are murdered in their home. The killer left few clues. It looked like a professional hit, and the investigation led nowhere. But the FBI refused to give up.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Threat
programme.desc: A suspected drug dealer would stop at nothing to keep local and federal authorities from building a case against him. When his organization started targeting the government itself, federal agencies went on high alert.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mork's Health Hints
programme.desc: Mindy goes to the hospital to have her tonsils removed, but a mix-up finds her being prepped for brain surgery.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: The Mork Syndrome
programme.desc: When Mork decides to join the air force, he unwittingly learns of the cover up of a radiation spill.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: The Night They Raided Mind-Ski's
programme.desc: Mork haplessly gets involved with a group of white supremacists.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Hold That Mork
programme.desc: Nelson tries to further his political career by getting Mork the opportunity to try out to be a Denver Broncos cheerleader.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Jeanie Loves Mork
programme.desc: Mindy takes a job writing the Miss Lonely Hearts newspaper column and unknowingly encourages Jean to pursue a romantic relationship with Mork.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: The Way Mork Were
programme.desc: Fred confesses that he's having marital difficulties, so Mork and Mindy relay stories of their troubled times.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Putting the Ork Back In Mork (Part 2)
programme.desc: It could be Mork's last night on Earth when an ancient Orkan elder who looks like a ten-year-old boy arrives with the news that Mork must return to Ork for forecycling because he has picked up too many Earthly ways.
programme.sub-title: Putting the Ork Back In Mork (Part 2)

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mork In Never-Never Land
programme.desc: With Mindy depressed about not getting a scholarship, it's up to Mork to stir up some laughs, so he brings home a friend who claims to be the real Peter Pan, with the power to fly as just one of his magical talents.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Dueling Skates
programme.desc: It's the biggest, funniest roller marathon ever when Mork takes to skates for the first time to race a cheating champion skater down a Rocky Mountain peak into Boulder to save the day care center at which he works from destruction.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mork the Prankster
programme.desc: Spaceman Mork gets so cosmically carried away with pulling practical jokes that he surprises Mindy by assembling her jeep in their living room right over Mr. Bickley's apartment - with disastrous results.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mork, The Monkey's Uncle
programme.desc: Mork turns the apartment he shares with Mindy into a hilarious hideaway for a young chimpanzee that he kidnaps from the Boulder zoo in the mistaken belief that the animal's mother has been abducted.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mork's New Look
programme.desc: Mork sends the face-lifting business into orbit when he hilariously rushes to a plastic surgeon to shoot the works after Mindy gives Mork the idea that everyone could use a little improverrent on Mork & Mindy.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mork and The Bum Rap
programme.desc: Naive Mork puts on his own out-of-this-world telethon to help Mindy raise charity funds, but is such a funny failure at fund-raising that he turns to his own outrageous brand of panhandling to come up with the needed money.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: There's A New Mork In Town
programme.desc: Mork faces almost unbeatable competition for his job as official Earth observer from his Orkan hero, the suave and charming Xerko, who beams to Earth to challenge Mork to an outrageous contest for the right to send reports back to Ork.
programme.sub-title: There's A New Mork In Town

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mork Meets Robin Williams
programme.desc: Mork, who has never heard of Robin Williams and scoffs at the idea of resembling him, gets crushed by mobs of fans who think he is the popular comedian, then has a hilarious nose-to-nose encounter with the famous star.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mindy, Mindy, Mindy
programme.desc: Mork, lonely in Mindy's absence, sets the stage for amazing comical confusion when he gets the visiting Orkan Elder to clone multiple Mindys which he continues to do until he gets it right.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mork The Swinging Single
programme.desc: Mindy is convinced that she has unleashed the swingingest single ever after she persuades Mork that it would inprove their relationship if each began dating new people and he turns into the hilarious new king of Boulder's social scene.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mork and The Family Reunion
programme.desc: When Mindy eats one of Mork's gourmet delights, she turns into a wacky Orkan herself, sending her family reunion into a rollicking tizzy and treating pompous old Uncle Dave (special guest star Jonathan Winters) to a bizarre evening he won't forget.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: I Heard It Through The Morkvine
programme.desc: Mork, who doesn't understand why people gossip, reveals some hilarious inside scoops when he shows and tells all about his friends while subbing for Mindy as host of a local TV show called Rumor Has It.
programme.sub-title: I Heard It Through The Morkvine

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mindy and Mork
programme.desc: When Mindy has to work long hours on a new TV call-in show, Mork has to help with the housework. But when his comedic cleaning efforts turn the apartment into a disaster area, he calls up Mindy, on the air, for a new set of instructions.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Regrets and Reflections
programme.desc: Mr. Bickley's 50th birthday celebration turns into a crazy exchange of everybody's deepest secrets, but Mindy waits until the group leaves to tell Mork somthing she's wanted to say for years.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: The Wedding
programme.desc: Mindy is the beautiful bride and Mork is the outrageously funny groom - who may not be able to fit into his tuxedo if he can't convince Orson to approve the marriage and undo his transformation of Mork into an English sheep dog.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Three The Hard Way
programme.desc: Mork and unsuspecting Mindy hatch a comedy plot that's bigger than both of them when an egg emerges from Mork's navel that grows and grows until it bursts open and out pops their full-grown, middle-aged-looking son.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Mama Mork, Papa Mindy
programme.desc: Mindy is dazed at the reality that she and Mork are the parents of Mearth -half-earthling/half-orkan with the body of a 50-year-old and the mind of an infant and feels lef t out when Mearth accepts Mork as his mommy.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: PS 2001
programme.desc: Mearth hates his first day of school on planet Ork so much that Mork and Mindy accompany him back to Ork Prep, where Mork's ancient teacher - who looks like a child - is still sternly in charge.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Metamorphosis - The TV Show
programme.desc: Mindy, already worried about losing her job at the television station, foresees disaster when Mork and Mearth accidentally transfer minds, forcing her to take Mearth in Mork’s body to meet the new boss.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Drive, She Said
programme.desc: Terror-stricken Mork believes the examiner assigned to give him a driving test is an incarnation of the devil, and careens along the test course in a spectacular tilted wheelie, with Mindy and Mearth in the car.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Midas Mork
programme.desc: All that glitters is Mork's gold when the Orkan learns how to make the precious metal from polyester, buys Mindy a 60-room mansion where she can stable her new horse and hires tennis star Tracy Austin as son Mearth's tennis coach.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Gotta Run (Part 1)
programme.desc: Mork is overjoyed that he and Mindy can finally let it all hang out when he meets Kalnik - an alien from Neptune who apparently is also married to an earthling and the four seem to have everything in common.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: When a dishonest antique dealer sells Aunt Sally to the Piwakawaka Folklore Museum in New Zealand, Worzel Gummidge just has to follow.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: The Crowman finds out about a rival scarecrow-maker who has made the scarecrow ingredient 'mangel-worzel' scarce. Meanwhile Worzel himself goes missing.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel finds he has competition in the scarecrow business and doesn't fare much better when he ends up trying his hand at being a clothes prop.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: After being unwittingly left behind at a golf course, Worzel gets caught up in the efforts of The Captain and Aunt Sally culminating in the Annual Golf Championship Competition.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel completes a postponed Scarecrow Naming Ceremony and finds himself acclaimed as 'King of The Scarecrows' - which the Crowman is less than thrilled to hear about.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary series following an Essex police unit.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A BMW ploughs into a house over the Christmas period.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary series. Police race to intercept three men suspected of robbing a string of plumbers' merchants. A fleeing van speeds the wrong way round a major roundabout (2/15).

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 1
programme.desc: Andy has a high-speed chase through a housing estate.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 2
programme.desc: Damo and the firearms team tackle a supermarket gunman.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 4
programme.desc: The hunt for an armed robber turns up a nasty surprise.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 8
programme.desc: One man claims that he has been driving with a dodgy numberplate for the past 18 years.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 1
programme.desc: The operational support unit tackles a daring drugs raid.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 12
programme.desc: Action-packed special highlighting some serious cases.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 6
programme.desc: The Interceptors respond to a fight involving a handgun.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: An Interceptor has a narrow escape.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 10
programme.desc: The interceptors are attacked by axe-wielding criminals.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 6
programme.desc: The officers face a 40-mile dash to prevent a potential suicide.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 9
programme.desc: Justin and Paul chase down a gang of suspected car thieves.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 4
programme.desc: The Interceptors come under attack from a gang of violent, armed robbers.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary series following an Essex police unit.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 3
programme.desc: The Lincolnshire law enforcers intercept an illegal car operation.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team hunts a wanted criminal as he flees on a motorbike.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 10
programme.desc: The team tackles some high-spirited boozers in Barrow.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: The legend of the werewolf throughout history and in various cultures is explored. The Conspiracy Show travels to England and rural Wisconsin to investigate claims of actual werewolf sightings.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)
programme.desc: The Allies defeated the Axis Powers on the battlefield, but did they actually win the war? Richard interviews several alternative historians who say that while the German Army may have surrendered in 1945, the Nazi's never officially did.
programme.sub-title: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A serious investigation of the long-held theory that Elvis Presley faked his death in August 1977. Jokes of Elvis sightings in Kalamazoo aside, there is a considerable volume of evidence to suggest Elvis may not have left the building

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Manchurian Candidates
programme.desc: Several prominent researchers in the field provide a short history of Mind Control experimentation beginning with the Nazis through to the CIA's infamous MK Ultra program.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Was Presidential hopeful, Robert F. Kennedy really killed by disgruntled Palestinian Sirhan Sirhan as we’ve been led to believe? Or was he another in a long line of Manchurian candidates, a victim of a long standing mind control program.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard witnesses a municipal employee in Toronto, Canada undergo a regression during which he re-experiences a previous life as a doctor in early 20 century England.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Does the Bible contain hidden messages encoded within certain passages of the Old Testament? Is the prophetic nature of these 'codes' proof the Bible's author is of supernatural or extraterrestrial origin?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard interviews the expedition organizer who is planning on leading a team of scientists, filmmakers and tourists to the Arctic aboard a Russian nuclear ice-breaker in search of an entrance into the interior of our world.
programme.sub-title: Journey to the Center of the Earth

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard meets members of the 9/11 Truth movement who challenge the official version of events concerning the attack on the U.S. Pentagon. Researchers present evidence to suggest the attack was perpetrated by some rogue element inside the U.S. government.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims that the supposed mastermind behind the 9/11 terrorist attacks and leader of the Al Qaeda terrorist cell remained a CIA asset until his death.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with two investigative journalists and a natural health practitioner who maintain vaccines are neither safe nor effective.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Can radiation from electrical appliances, power lines, microwave ovens, and wireless technology such as cell phones, baby monitors, alter our DNA, and cause illnesses?
programme.sub-title: The Dangers of EMF (Electro Magnetic Frequencies)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Alien Abductions
programme.desc: Richard speaks with two alleged alien abductees about their claims they were taken aboard an alien space craft.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: For centuries, farmers have been discovering strange, complex geometric patterns comprised of rings, circles and other intricate shapes in their fields.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard meets the owners of an abnormal humanoid skull allegedly discovered in Mexico in the 1930s and a researcher who has had the skull subjected to numerous tests including carbon-14 dating, and DNA in order to determine it’s origin.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Mystery of Oak Island
programme.desc: Richard investigates Canada's best-known mysteries, the Oak Island treasure. Also known as the money pit, the site has become one of the world's deepest and most costly archaeological digs.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Energy Healing
programme.desc: Several energy healers claim they are able to produce spontaneous miraculous healing through a laying on of hands. Claims of similar such healing by a man in a remote village in Brazil, known as John of God are also examined.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Execution of MLK and The Framing of James Earl Ray
programme.desc: In this re-examination of the assassination of civil rights leader Martin Luther King, Richard meets several researchers who provide evidence that the convicted gunman, James Earl Ray was not the real triggerman.
programme.sub-title: The Execution of MLK and The Framing of James Earl Ray

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Birthers - Obama's Birth Certificate
programme.desc: Richard interviews several prominent 'Birthers' who present evidence that proves Barack Obama is not eligible to be President because he does not meet the qualifications of a natural born citizen.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: RFID Chips
programme.desc: Could implantable RFID chips or Radio-frequency identification tags currently in widespread use in retail industry be used to surreptitiously track and control people?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Assassination of John Lennon
programme.desc: The Conspiracy Show travels to New York, LA and Toronto in order to investigate the murder of John Lennon. Several researchers posit that Lennon's supposed killer Mark David Chapman was either a patsy or a mind-controlled assassin.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Did Jim Morrison Fake His Death?
programme.desc: Is it conceivable that the Doors legendary front man did not die in a bathtub in Paris of an apparent drug overdose?
programme.sub-title: Did Jim Morrison Fake His Death?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Water Engine
programme.desc: Did the late Stanley Meyer figure out a way to run an internal combustion engine on pure water? And was he murdered because of the threat his invention posed to Big Oil?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Murder of Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Did this pop icon and blond bombshell succumb to a drug overdose in 1962 as we've been led to believe or was she killed for 'knowing too much?"

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Is The Cure for Cancer Being Suppressed?
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims that the medical profession, drug companies, the food industry and government agencies are suppressing a cure for cancer.
programme.sub-title: Is The Cure for Cancer Being Suppressed?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Bilderbergs
programme.desc: A glimpse backstage at the highly secretive annual gathering of the world's richest and most powerful movers and shakers.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Shroud of Turin
programme.desc: Is this famous piece of linen cloth the actual burial cloth of Jesus Christ or a clever Medieval hoax?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Fake Arab Spring?
programme.desc: Who was really behind a series of revolts, protests, and uprisings that swept the Middle East starting in late 2010?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Ouija Boards?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Global Warming, A Hoax?
programme.desc: Richard speaks with several journalists and researchers who argue the theory that human use of carbon-based fossil fuels will lead to catastrophic global warming.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: JFK
programme.desc: Richard meets with some prominent JFK assassination researchers to discuss the November 1963 murder of President John F. Kennedy.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Pterodactyls
programme.desc: Richard speaks with several explorers and cryptozoologists who believe pterodactyls, flying dinosaurs thought to have become extinct some 60 million years ago, are alive and thriving in remote places such as Papua New Guinea.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: GMO - Genetically Modified Food
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims crop plants created for human or animal consumption using the latest molecular biology techniques are unsafe.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Fluoride
programme.desc: The Conspiracy Show investigates the safety and efficacy of putting fluoride in our water supply. Dentists say that putting fluoride in the water supply prevents tooth decay, but some researchers claim it's a toxic substance that causes cancer.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Area 51
programme.desc: What's going on inside Area 51, America's most carefully guarded secret? The mysterious military base located 30 minutes north west of Las Vegas has been rumored to be involved in back engineering advanced alien technology recovered from UFO crash sites.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Remote Viewing
programme.desc: We meet several individuals who claim they have the ability to transcend time and space and identify people, objects, and locations hidden from their view.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Freemasons

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Hell Hath No Fury
programme.desc: When George Long's girlfriend demands they get married, he breaks off the relationship. But nothing can stop Cheryl's relentless obsession. If she can't marry him, nobody can.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Web of Destruction
programme.desc: Mai Hlee Xiong is a beauty pageant contestant fresh out of college. A stalker is watching her every move and he'll stop at nothing to defame her good reputation.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Devil in Disguise
programme.desc: Sandi Musk was looking for a partner when she meets a young man with a violent streak. When Sandi tries to call off the relationship, she is in for a nightmare beyond belief.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: A Mother's Nightmare
programme.desc: When Jessica takes out a restraining order against her ex-husband, he vows to get her back. When police won't protect her, Jessica is left to face the horrors that will change her life forever.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Friends and Neighbors
programme.desc: June 6th, 1998. Six-year-old Brooke Sutton wakes up to find her grandmother murdered in her small-town Ohio home. Covered in blood herself, Brooke runs to a neighbor for help. Investigators are called to the scene.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Who Took Polly Klaas?
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Polly Klaas and two friends are having a sleepover and playing board games, when a man brandishing a knife appears at the door, ties them up, nabs Polly, and then disappears into the night.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Framed
programme.desc: In Ada Oklahoma, 21-year-old Debbie Sue Carter is found raped, beaten, and strangled in her apartment. It’s a horrific scene; a belt around her neck, a bloody palm print by her bed, cryptic notes scrawled in ketchup, and nail polish on the walls.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Queen of Hearts
programme.desc: In Lynwood, Washington, 1979, Bill Hassler returns home to find his high school sweetheart, 24-year-old Susan Schwarz, naked under a towel, hands bound, and shot three times in the head.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Close to Home
programme.desc: A fifteen-year-old girl goes to deliver a letter for her parents. Only a few hours later, she is found viciously murdered and left under a slide in a park, only 120 yards from her home. Ansley Common, in Nuneaton, Warwickshire is a close-knit village.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Blood on the Snow
programme.desc: On the morning of January 31st, 1969, a young woman is assaulted, stabbed, and left to die in a snow bank. The town of Saskatoon is horrified. It’s just the latest in a recent rash of murders and rapes.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Cross Dressed to Kill
programme.desc: December 26, 2000 - Cassia County, Idaho. Kacy Kay Ray leaves her parents’ home after celebrating Christmas and vanishes. Days later, her frozen body is found in a field bludgeoned and stabbed, the victim of a gruesome rape.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Nightmares in Babysitting
programme.desc: Fourteen-year-old Karen Slattery is brutally stabbed and raped while babysitting in South Florida’s upscale Delray Beach. Police follow up on dozens of tips but the case goes cold.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: The Lady Killer
programme.desc: Investigators find the parallels between the two cases troubling: two young, isolated and vulnerable Guyanese women, two deaths in strange circumstances, two life insurance policies. Will Mohammed Khan escape justice again?

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime
programme.desc: After finding the body of a young girl in a stream located in the suburbs of Montreal, investigators strive to retrace a murderer who knew how to use water to his advantage.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: The Abbotsford Killer
programme.desc: Two girls are attacked by a man armed with a baseball bat. One of them manages to escape but the second one is found in a salmon river. The killer nags the police for months until he leaves behind a telling clue.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: The Mystery of Disembodied Feet
programme.desc: The summer of 2007, a young girl walks on the beach and a human foot in a sneaker. Less than a week later, a couple discovers another running shoe with a foot inside of it south of there. Thus begins the mystery of the disembodied feet.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Mortal Passion
programme.desc: In early April 2000, two students discover the lifeless body of a young woman on the edge of a river. The victim has wounds on her neck, but a murder weapon is not found at the scene. Investigators find a piece of paper in the victim’s pant pocket.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Scenario for a Murder
programme.desc: Since October 10th 2008, no-one has seen friendly 38 year-old bachelor John Altinger. All that his friends know is that he was going to a garage in a residential area of Edmonton, to meet a woman he’d met on an online dating site.

programme.title: Naked Science: What's Wrong With Pluto
programme.desc: Naked Science investigates the violent cosmological forces that create planets and discover what it is that makes a planet a planet.

programme.title: Naked Science: The Great Flood
programme.desc: Almost every culture on Earth tells the story of an ancient flood – details vary but the basic plot is the same - An angry God wipes out an unrepentant population.

programme.title: Naked Science: Making Mountains
programme.desc: The Rockies. Magnificent and awe-inspiring mysterious, the Rockies are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world. Naked Science investigates the geological processes that build these massive mountains.

programme.title: Naked Science: Diamonds
programme.desc: Naked Science follows the story of a diamond from the creation of carbon inside the heart of a dying sun, through to the cutting of a diamond mine in South Africa.

programme.title: Naked Science: Anatomy Of Tornado
programme.desc: Tornados are one of the most destructive forces of the planets with winds at 300 miles an hour. They can destroy buildings and hurl cars hundreds of feet through the air.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Scoreless
programme.desc: Using a unique method to crack a safe, Nick Wells (Robert De Niro) uses a blowtorch to cut through a safe, fill it with water.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Just-Terry-Fied
programme.desc: Terry and Larry will be test 2 scenes from Justified where, Terry finds himself in a bathtub and a dead body lying over top of him.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Leftovers Again?

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Terry's Criminal Minds
programme.desc: A murderer tries to kill his final victim as the car they are in slowly sinks to the bottom of a lake.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Terry Down Under
programme.desc: Terry and Larry start off the new season testing one of the most requested films, Quigley Down Under.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: The Harder Terry Falls
programme.desc: Dust off your chaps as Terry tests several things from the epic western The Harder They Fall, and a canteen buster test from Gunsmoke!

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Bond, Terry Bond
programme.desc: Terry and Larry will be testing the effectiveness of driving a vehicle with fully automatic machine guns mounted to the car!

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Saving Private Terry
